heading: Disposal
label: 904 Disposal
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)904(a), (b)40:532., title VIII, §§ 803, 806(c), as added , , .June 30, 1949, ch. 288Pub. L. 90–577, title V, § 50182 Stat. 1105Oct. 16, 1968904(c)40:535(c).In subsection (a), the words “Whenever the Administrator contemplates the disposal for or on behalf of any Federal agency of” and “such land” are omitted as unnecessary. The words “the head of the governing body of” are omitted for consistency in the chapter. The words “exercising zoning and land-use jurisdiction” are substituted for “having jurisdiction over zoning and land-use regulation in the geographical area within which the land or lands are located” to eliminate unnecessary words and for consistency in the chapter.In subsection (c)(2), the word “Preparing” is added for clarity.

L. 90–577, title V, § 50182 Stat. 1105Oct. 16, 1968904(c)40:535(c).In subsection (a), the words “Whenever the Administrator contemplates the disposal for or on behalf of any Federal agency of” and “such land” are omitted as unnecessary. The words “the head of the governing body of” are omitted for consistency in the chapter. The words “exercising zoning and land-use jurisdiction” are substituted for “having jurisdiction over zoning and land-use regulation in the geographical area within which the land or lands are located” to eliminate unnecessary words and for consistency in the chapter.In subsection (c)(2), the word “Preparing” is added for clarity.

children.heading: .—Notice to Local Government
children.label: a .—Notice to Local Government
children.text: Before offering real property situated in an urban area for sale, the Administrator of General Services shall give reasonable notice to the unit of general local government exercising zoning and land use jurisdiction in order to provide an opportunity for zoning so that the property is used in accordance with local comprehensive planning described in subsection (c).
current zoning regulations, prospective zoning requirements, and objectives for property if it is unzoned; and

children.label: A
children.text: the current availability of streets, sidewalks, sewers, water, street lights, and other service facilities; and

children.label: B
children.text: the prospective availability of those service facilities if the property is included in local comprehensive planning described in subsection (c).

children.label: A
children.text: the pattern and intensity of land use;

children.label: B
children.text: the provision of public facilities (including transportation facilities) and other government services; and

children.label: C
children.text: the effective development and use of human and natural resources.
Preparing long-range physical and fiscal plans for government action.

Programming capital improvements and other major expenditures, based on a determination of relative urgency, together with definitive financial planning for expenditures in the earlier years of a program.
Coordinating related plans and activities of state and local governments and agencies.
Preparing regulatory and administrative measures to support activities described in this subsection.
